title: Crisp Umami Tripe Stir-fry
description: A low-carb-friendly Algerian dish featuring crispy tripe stir-fried with a blend of umami-rich spices and bhuna-style sauce to help ease hangovers.

ingredients.name: tripe
ingredients.notes: ensure it is fresh and properly cleaned

ingredients.name: tomato puree
ingredients.notes: adjust to taste with water or vegetable broth

ingredients.name: bhuna spices blend
ingredients.notes: blend of cumin, coriander, turmeric, and other preferred spices

ingredients.name: tahini sauce
ingredients.notes: adjust to taste with water or lemon juice for a creamy texture

ingredients.name: lemon juice
ingredients.notes: optional for tanginess and color enhancement

ingredients.name: salt
ingredients.notes: use sea salt for better flavor

instructions: Rinse tripe under cold water, then soak it in a solution of white vinegar and warm water to remove any impurities. Pat dry.
Heat a large skillet over medium heat. Sauté chopped onion and minced garlic until translucent and golden brown.
Add grated ginger along with bhuna spices blend and stir-fry for 1-2 minutes, allowing the spices to release their flavors into the oil.
Push aside the vegetable mixture and add tripe directly into the hot skillet. Cook in a single layer without overcrowding; this helps achieve crispy texture.
Stir in tomato puree, salt, and lemon juice (optional). Allow the sauce to simmer for about 5-7 minutes until it starts thickening slightly.
Gradually incorporate tahini sauce while stirring continuously to ensure even cooking of tripe. Adjust seasoning with additional salt or vinegar if necessary.
Once done, remove from heat and serve hot as a light lunch dish. Garnish with fresh herbs for added freshness.
